show_of_the_month.title: Louise Bourgeois
show_of_the_month.short_description: The "Gathering Wool" exhibition showcases Louise Bourgeois's exploration of abstraction through late and previously unseen works, highlighting her consistent themes of psychological emotion and childhood trauma across her seven-decade career.

top_5_seen.title: Rob Pruitt
top_5_seen.short_description: 303 Gallery presents Rob Pruitt's third solo exhibition, featuring time-based works that explore color gradients and capture fleeting moments. Daily changing titles reflect the evolving nature of time. The show also includes a video by Jonathan Horowitz addressing hyper-masculine identity.

top_5_seen.title: Anish Kapoor
top_5_seen.short_description: Anish Kapoor's exhibition showcases his reflective stainless-steel sculptures and painted mirrors, exploring themes of spatial illusion and viewer perception. This collection highlights his innovative approach to form and materiality. The show offers a rich opportunity to experience Kapoor's influential artistic legacy.

top_5_seen.title: Louise Bourgeois
top_5_seen.short_description: The "Gathering Wool" exhibition showcases Louise Bourgeois's exploration of abstraction through late and previously unseen works, highlighting her consistent themes of psychological emotion and childhood trauma across her seven-decade career.

top_5_seen.title: Group Show
top_5_seen.short_description: Craig Starr Gallery presents an exhibition featuring Stanley Whitney's previously unexhibited works alongside pieces by Henri Matisse. Both artists emphasize color as a structural element in their compositions. A fully illustrated catalogue accompanies the show, including insights from John Elderfield.

top_5_seen.title: Jon Burgerman
top_5_seen.venue_name: A Hug From The Art World
top_5_seen.short_description: A Hug From The Art World presents Jon Burgerman's first New York solo exhibition, featuring twenty new paintings exploring themes of family and connection. The exhibition opens on January 22nd and runs until March 7th. Burgerman's work combines whimsical elements with deeper emotional complexities.

top_5_saved.title: mother
top_5_saved.short_description: SEIZAN Gallery's upcoming exhibition, "MOTHER," features artists exploring motherhood through diverse perspectives, showcasing works by Marina Berio, Yukiko Hata, Eri Iwasaki, Miné Okubo, and Asako Tabata. The show runs from February 12 to March 14.

top_5_saved.title: our neighborhood: another look around
top_5_saved.short_description: Hannah Traore Gallery's exhibition, "Our Neighborhood: Another Look Around," showcases artwork by Lower East Side elementary students, emphasizing community engagement and the importance of art in education. Proceeds will support local schools.

top_5_saved.title: amazonia açu
top_5_saved.short_description: Americas Society presents "Amazonia Açu," showcasing over fifty works by thirty-four Amazonian artists, aiming to challenge misconceptions about the region's cultural diversity while promoting dialogue on contemporary issues. The exhibition runs from September 3, 2025, to April 18, 2026.

top_5_saved.title: Deborah Roberts
top_5_saved.short_description: Deborah Roberts' exhibition "Consequences of Being" features paintings, works on paper, and her debut ceramic sculpture, exploring colonialism's impacts on Black identity and culture from diverse global perspectives.

top_5_saved.title: Il Lee
top_5_saved.short_description: Il Lee, a prominent abstract artist, utilizes ballpoint pens to create dynamic compositions that invite viewers into a transformative dialogue, continuing the legacy of abstract art in New York City.

top_5_views.title: Rob Pruitt
top_5_views.short_description: 303 Gallery presents Rob Pruitt's third solo exhibition, featuring time-based works that explore color gradients and capture fleeting moments. Daily changing titles reflect the evolving nature of time. The show also includes a video by Jonathan Horowitz addressing hyper-masculine identity.

top_5_views.title: Glenn Ligon
top_5_views.short_description: Glenn Ligon's exhibition, "Late at night, early in the morning, at noon," features new and historic works exploring language, abstraction, and the color blue, reflecting on emotional and cultural themes through text and image. The show combines large-scale new works and prints spanning three decades, highlighting Ligon's evolution in printmaking and engagement with American history and literature. By intertwining Baldwin's writings with visual art, Ligon evokes deeper emotional truths and historical reflections.

top_5_views.title: Group Show
top_5_views.short_description: Craig Starr Gallery presents an exhibition featuring Stanley Whitney's previously unexhibited works alongside pieces by Henri Matisse. Both artists emphasize color as a structural element in their compositions. A fully illustrated catalogue accompanies the show, including insights from John Elderfield.

top_5_views.title: nightswimming
top_5_views.short_description: The exhibition "Nightswimming," curated by Erica Samuels at Broadway, features works that explore themes of memory and introspection, inspired by R.E.M.'s song. Artists investigate the balance of danger and serenity, inviting viewers to reflect on inner experiences. The show emphasizes art's role in navigating private moments of clarity.

top_5_views.title: Nicolas Party
top_5_views.short_description: Nicolas Party's exhibition, "Dead Fish," features oil-on-copper and pastel artworks that reinterpret historical themes and engage with traditional painting techniques, blending past and present.
